result_summary_json.resolved_query: Prepare approximately 15 Beamer slides covering the referenced paper for an ML research audience.

args_redacted_json.instruction: Prepare approximately 15 Beamer slides covering the referenced paper for an ML research audience.

result_summary_json.contribution: Introduces Action-aware Dynamic Pruning (ADP) for Vision-Language-Action models, a framework that dynamically toggles visual token pruning based on the robot's recent action trajectory, thereby accelerating inference while maintaining or improving manipulation success rates.
result_summary_json.method: ADP utilizes text-to-vision attention scores to rank and select the top-K most relevant visual tokens. It introduces a gating mechanism that computes the cumulative arc distance of the end-effector's recent trajectory to distinguish between coarse and fine-grained manipulation phases. Pruning is adaptively applied only during coarse, high-velocity phases where visual redundancy is high, preserving full visual context during precise, fine-grained operations.
key_results: Significantly reduces FLOPs and action inference latency, demonstrating a 1.35x speedup on OpenVLA-OFT.
Yields up to a 25.8% improvement in manipulation success rates with OpenVLA on the LIBERO simulation suites compared to baselines.
Demonstrates robustness and efficiency in diverse real-world scenarios on the Jaco2 hardware platform.
key_equations: s_{i+1} = \begin{cases} 1, & \delta_i \ge U^{(i)}, \\ 0, & \delta_i \le V^{(i)}, \\ s_i, & V^{(i)} < \delta_i < U^{(i)}. \end{cases}

args_redacted_json.slide_title: 針對視覺-語言-動作模型之動作感知動態剪枝技術
視覺-語言-動作 (VLA) 模型的計算瓶頸

result_summary_json.frame: \begin{frame} \frametitle{Computational Bottlenecks in VLA Models} \begin{itemize} \item Massive visual tokens from high-resolution inputs \item Severe inference latency from excessive FLOPs \item High-frequency response needed for closed-loop control \item Static models fail strict latency constraints \end{itemize} \end{frame}

args_redacted_json.slide_title: 機器人操作過程中的階段性與視覺冗餘

result_summary_json.frame: \begin{frame}{Phased Characteristics and Visual Redundancy} \begin{itemize} \item Tasks divide into Coarse and Fine-grained manipulation phases. \item Coarse movements exhibit high visual redundancy and fault tolerance. \item Fine-grained operations require precise visual details for success. \end{itemize} \begin{center} \includegraphics[width=0.85\textwidth,height=0.7\textheight,keepaspectratio]{p0-fig-001} \end{center} \end{frame}

視覺標記的 Top-K 篩選機制

result_summary_json.frame: \begin{frame}{Top-K Selection for Visual Tokens} \begin{itemize} \item Rank visual tokens by computed attention scores. \item Retain top $K$ tokens via retention ratio $\rho$. \end{itemize} \begin{equation*} \mathbf{X}_{\text{keep}} = \operatorname{Top-K}\big(\Phi^{(l)}, k\big), \quad k = \lfloor \rho \cdot L^{\text{vis}} \rfloor \end{equation*} \begin{itemize} \item Discard background redundancy to significantly reduce computation. \end{itemize} \end{frame}

實驗結果：推論加速與運算量降低

result_summary_json.frame: \begin{frame}\frametitle{Experimental Results: Inference Speedup \& Computation Reduction}\begin{itemize}\item Significant FLOPs reduction achieved on the OpenVLA-OFT model.\item Action inference latency drops, achieving a 1.35x overall speedup.\item Overcomes real-time VLA bottlenecks without hardware modifications.\end{itemize}\end{frame}

args_redacted_json.slide_title: 實驗結果：LIBERO 模擬環境操作成功率

result_summary_json.frame: \begin{frame} \frametitle{Experimental Results: LIBERO Simulation Success Rate} \begin{itemize} \item Rigorously compared ADP against baselines on LIBERO simulation suite. \item Achieved up to 25.8\% higher success rate over OpenVLA baseline. \item Filters visual noise effectively during early movement phases. \item Retains crucial detailed features during critical manipulation stages. \end{itemize} \end{frame}
